title: fraxinus excelsior f. crispa
intro: Fraxinus excelsior f. crispa, also known as the Ash tree, is a deciduous tree that is a variant of the common ash tree (Fraxinus excelsior) with crinkled or curled leaves. The bark and leaves of Fraxinus excelsior f. crispa have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Fraxinus excelsior f. crispa include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pain, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, and its extracts can also be used in topical creams and salves to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
